mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, leading to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, housing starts act as a leading indicator of credit-sensitive demand; a sustained increase in HOUST signals resilient consumer credit conditions that support equity valuations despite higher discount rates.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to correlate with positive SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the conditional forward return spread between high and low HOUST periods reverses sign during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as earnings revisions propagate.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.05), or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for earnings seasonality.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.2% annualized, inflation expectations become embedded in pricing, prompting the Fed to pause rate hikes and allowing equity multiples to expand over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.2% threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with declining unemployment confirms broad-based labor market recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 60-day returns, or the joint effect reverses sign during periods of high market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions, industrial production becomes the dominant driver of corporate margins; sustained INDPRO growth signals supply normalization, supporting equity returns over the next month despite flat demand.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive SPX returns when GDPC1 is stagnant, or the signal's predictive rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.
